nodes.description: Abnormal tone is common, with both hypotonia and hypertonia reported in the EZH2-positive cohort.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: camptodactyly of the fingers and/or toes, umbilical hernia, abnormal tone, and hoarse, low cry in infancy.

nodes.description: Markedly advanced bone age (accelerated osseous maturation), a hallmark of Weaver syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: a range of associated clinical features including advanced bone age, poor coordination, soft, doughy skin, camptodactyly

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotypic and Genotypic Spectrum of Weaver Syndrome: A Missense Variant of the EZH2 Gene.
evidence.snippet: ocular hypertelorism, a broad forehead, almond-shaped palpebral fissures

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotypic and Genotypic Spectrum of Weaver Syndrome: A Missense Variant of the EZH2 Gene.
evidence.snippet: The characteristic facial features are ocular hypertelorism, a broad forehead, almond-shaped palpebral fissures

nodes.description: Camptodactyly of the fingers and/or toes; a recurrent feature useful for stratifying individuals to EZH2 testing.

evidence.reference_title: Weaver syndrome and EZH2 mutations: Clarifying the clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Additional clinical features which may help in stratifying individuals to EZH2 mutation testing include camptodactyly, soft, doughy skin, umbilical hernia, and a low, hoarse cry.

nodes.description: Characteristic facial gestalt: ocular hypertelorism, broad forehead, almond-shaped palpebral fissures, large fleshy ears in early childhood, retrognathia, and a pointed "stuck-on" chin with horizontal skin creases. Features can be subtle and age-dependent.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotypic and Genotypic Spectrum of Weaver Syndrome: A Missense Variant of the EZH2 Gene.
evidence.snippet: The characteristic facial features are ocular hypertelorism, a broad forehead, almond-shaped palpebral fissures and, in early childhood, large, fleshy ears, a pointed "stuck-on" chin with horizontal skin creases, and retrognathia.

nodes.description: In bone, perturbed H3K27 methylation balance dysregulates osteoblast differentiation and the BMP signaling pathway, driving excess osteogenesis, skeletal overgrowth, and advanced bone age. A knock-in mouse model of the common EZH2 p.R684C variant shows global H3K27me3 depletion, abnormal bone parameters indicative of skeletal overgrowth, and increased osteogenic activity, with reciprocal balance restorable by inhibiting the opposing H3K27 demethylases KDM6A/KDM6B.
nodes.id: Dysregulated Osteoblast Differentiation and Skeletal Overgrowth

evidence.reference_title: A mouse model of Weaver syndrome displays overgrowth and excess osteogenesis reversible with KDM6A/6B inhibition.
evidence.snippet: Ezh2R684C/+ mice had abnormal bone parameters, indicative of skeletal overgrowth, and Ezh2R684C/+ osteoblasts showed increased osteogenic activity.

evidence.reference_title: A mouse model of Weaver syndrome displays overgrowth and excess osteogenesis reversible with KDM6A/6B inhibition.
evidence.snippet: RNA-Seq comparing osteoblasts differentiated from Ezh2R684C/+, and Ezh2+/+ BM-mesenchymal stem cells (BM-MSCs) indicated collective dysregulation of the BMP pathway and osteoblast differentiation.

nodes.description: EZH2 is the catalytic subunit of Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2), the predominant enzyme that mono-, di-, and trimethylates histone H3 lysine 27 (H3K27me1/2/3). Heterozygous germline pathogenic EZH2 variants in Weaver syndrome are predominantly missense and impair PRC2 catalytic function. In vitro reconstitution shows WS-associated amino-acid changes reduce EZH2 histone methyltransferase activity, and isogenic stem-cell modeling indicates many variants act through dominant-negative interference with PRC2 even at low expression, lowering global H3K27me2/3 with reciprocal gains in H3K27ac.

evidence.reference_title: Germline mutations in the oncogene EZH2 cause Weaver syndrome and increased human height.
evidence.snippet: We performed exome sequencing in four individuals with Weaver syndrome, identifying a mutation in the histone methyltransferase, EZH2, in each case.

evidence.reference_title: Weaver syndrome-associated EZH2 protein variants show impaired histone methyltransferase function in vitro.
evidence.snippet: We found that WS-associated amino acid alterations reduce the histone methyltransferase function of EZH2 in this in vitro assay.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant-negative effects of Weaver syndrome-associated EZH2 variants.
evidence.snippet: We isogenically modeled 10 representative variants in embryonic stem cells and showed that they reduce global H3K27me2/3 with concomitant increases in H3K27ac and chromatin decompaction.

nodes.description: Reduced PRC2-mediated H3K27 methylation (with chromatin decompaction and loss of canonical PRC1 occupancy) impairs facultative heterochromatin formation and Polycomb-mediated transcriptional silencing. WS-associated EZH2 variants derepress weakly Polycomb-bound genes, including phenotypically relevant growth control genes, providing a mechanistic link between dysregulated histone modification and human overgrowth.
nodes.id: Impaired Polycomb Repression and Derepression of Growth Genes

evidence.reference_title: Dominant-negative effects of Weaver syndrome-associated EZH2 variants.
evidence.snippet: RNA-seq identified weakly Polycomb-bound genes that lose canonical PRC1 (cPRC1) occupancy and become derepressed, including several phenotypically relevant growth control genes.

evidence.reference_title: Germline mutations in the oncogene EZH2 cause Weaver syndrome and increased human height.
evidence.snippet: Our data establish EZH2 mutations as the cause of Weaver syndrome and provide further links between histone modifications and regulation of human growth.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is present in approximately 80% of affected individuals but is highly variable and frequently mild; intellect can range from normal to severe.

evidence.reference_title: Weaver syndrome and EZH2 mutations: Clarifying the clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Intellectual disability is also common, present in ~80%, but is highly variable and frequently mild.

nodes.description: Prenatal overgrowth; affected babies may be large at birth, prompting anticipatory delivery planning.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: Families and their health care providers should be made aware that an affected baby may be large so that appropriate delivery plans can be made.

nodes.description: A low, hoarse cry in infancy is a recognizable feature of Weaver syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: abnormal tone, and hoarse, low cry in infancy.

nodes.description: Enlarged head circumference is a recurrent feature.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: EZH2-related overgrowth is a variable overgrowth syndrome characterized by tall stature, macrocephaly, variable intellect

nodes.description: Neuroblastoma occurs at increased frequency in individuals with a heterozygous EZH2 pathogenic variant; it is the most commonly reported tumor type in Weaver syndrome. There is currently no clear evidence of an increased frequency of additional malignancies.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: Neuroblastoma occurs at an increased frequency in individuals with a heterozygous EZH2 pathogenic variant.

nodes.description: There are no internationally ratified neuroblastoma surveillance guidelines for EZH2 variant carriers, but US guidelines recommend imaging and urine biochemistry surveillance until age 10 years; clinicians should maintain a low threshold for investigating possible tumor-related symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: US guidelines recommend imaging and urine biochemistry surveillance until age 10 years.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: a range of associated clinical features including advanced bone age, poor coordination, soft, doughy skin

nodes.description: Retrognathia with a pointed "stuck-on" chin and horizontal chin crease helps distinguish Weaver from Sotos syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the Phenotypic and Genotypic Spectrum of Weaver Syndrome: A Missense Variant of the EZH2 Gene.
evidence.snippet: a pointed "stuck-on" chin with horizontal skin creases, and retrognathia.

nodes.description: Soft, doughy skin is a recurrent feature of Weaver syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Weaver syndrome and EZH2 mutations: Clarifying the clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Additional clinical features which may help in stratifying individuals to EZH2 mutation testing include camptodactyly, soft, doughy skin, umbilical hernia, and a low, hoarse cry.

nodes.description: Toe camptodactyly may occasionally require surgical release.

evidence.reference_title: EZH2-Related Overgrowth.
evidence.snippet: Occasionally, toe camptodactyly may require surgical release.

nodes.description: Pre- and postnatal generalized overgrowth with tall stature, the most consistent feature, reported in >90% of EZH2 mutation-positive individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Weaver syndrome and EZH2 mutations: Clarifying the clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: tall stature is very common, reported in >90% of affected individuals.

nodes.description: Umbilical hernia is a recurrent feature of Weaver syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Weaver syndrome and EZH2 mutations: Clarifying the clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Additional clinical features which may help in stratifying individuals to EZH2 mutation testing include camptodactyly, soft, doughy skin, umbilical hernia, and a low, hoarse cry.
